title: Dortmanna sonchifolia
taxonomy: Dortmanna sonchifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a small genus of plants. Dortmanna sonchifolia is further classified as a member of the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Dortmanna sonchifolia is a part of the clade Angiosperms. Further classification places it within the superkingdom Embryophyta.

constituents: Dortmanna sonchifolia, also known as Creeping Sneezeweed, contains various active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, such as sonchiferol and sonchiferine, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. side_effects: Dortmanna sonchifolia, also known as wormwood or Chinese wormwood, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits. However, long-term consumption or high doses of Dortmanna sonchifolia can lead to negative side effects such as liver damage, stomach upset, and allergic reactions. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications and worsen underlying health conditions. It's also essential to note that pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid consuming Dortmanna sonchifolia due to its potential toxicity. Furthermore, individuals with liver disease or impaired liver function should consult a healthcare professional before using this plant medicinally.
intro: Dortmanna sonchifolia, also known as common dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Cuscutaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and diabetes. Bioactive constituents present in Dortmanna sonchifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices.
